✦
AI Priorities · Age 22 · Pupillage → Junior Lawyer
1 Emergency fund → 6 months expenses
Pupillage pay is low and unpredictable. Before optimising returns, lock in RM 15K (6 mo × RM 2.5K) in your Emergency Fund. You're at RM 3.3K — RM 11.7K gap. This is survival money, not investment.
2 Max out EPF voluntary contribution
Once you're a junior lawyer with stable income, contribute RM 250/mo extra to EPF i-Saraan. At 22, the tax relief + compounding over 38 years is the single highest-ROI move you can make. Free money from gov matching.
3 Shift gold overweight → index funds
Gold at 20% is defensive — great for 50-year-olds, but at 22 with a 38-year horizon, you want growth. Trim gold to 10% and redirect RM 4K into VOO/QQQ. Your age is your edge — use it.
4 Build a "Career Capital" fund
Junior lawyers earn RM 2.5–4K starting but jump to RM 8–15K by year 3–5. Set aside RM 200/mo for CLP prep, bar memberships, and networking. Income growth > savings rate at this stage — invest in yourself.
5 FIRE timeline: ramp savings with income
Your current 69% save rate is excellent but unsustainable on pupillage pay. Plan to save 50% of net income once you're earning RM 5K+. If you invest RM 2.5K/mo at 8% from age 24, you hit Coast FIRE (RM 229K) by age 30 and full FIRE by 42. Start strong, compound forever.
